sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Check wall boundaries after clicking bottom blue
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Current wall boundaries:")
for r in [1, 10, 20, 27, 28, 32, 40, 50, 63]:
    line = ascii_lines[r]
    transitions = []
    for c in range(63):
        if line[c] != line[c+1]:
            transitions.append((c, line[c], line[c+1]))
    if transitions:
        print(f"  Row {r}: {transitions}")

# Check what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame
before_lines = before.ascii.split('\n')
changed_rows = set()
for r in range(64):
    if before_lines[r] != ascii_lines[r]:
        changed_rows.add(r)
print(f"\nChanged rows: {sorted(changed_rows)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- Row 50: wall edge at col 11 (W at cols 4-11, G at 12+)
- Row 40: wall edge at col 43 (was 44)
So the bottom blue square swaps the wall edges in the "between barriers" section (rows 24-39) and the "bottom" section (rows 44-63). The between-barriers wall moved from 11 to 7, and the bottom wall moved from 7 to 11.
Also, the barrier-row wall edge moved from 44 to 43.
Let me track this more carefully. The changed rows are 24-39 and 44-63, which are the sections below the first barrier. The upper section (rows 1-19) didn't change.
So the bottom blue square affects the sections below the first barrier. It seems to shift the wall boundaries in a specific pattern.
